sn10.9:1.1:
1
On one occasion the Blessed One was dwelling at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the Squirrel Sanctuary. Now on that occasion the bhikkhuni Sukka, surrounded by a large assembly, was teaching the Dhamma. Then a yakkha who had full confidence in the bhikkhuni Sukka, going from street to street and from square to square in Rājagaha, on that occasion recited these verses:
sn10.9:1.2:
2
"What has happened to these people in Rājagaha?
They sleep as if they’ve been drinking mead.
Why don’t they attend on Sukka
As she teaches the deathless state?
3
"But the wise, as it were, drink it up—
That
(Dhamma)
irresistible,
Ambrosial, nutritious—
As travellers do a cloud."
